main_conclusion: The bottleneck is best framed as a selective marginal-allocation problem rather than ordinary routing or plain binary pair classification.
core_shift: Move away from brittle binary pairwise winner labels and toward budget-conditioned marginal continuation value with an explicit unresolved/defer region.
important_points: branch-level budget-conditioned value is more faithful than forced pairwise wins
pairwise decisions should increasingly be derived from value separation
hardest pairs should not always be forced into a total order
raw predicted magnitude is not automatically confidence
existing data should be reused more intelligently before broad new data expansion
practical_experiment_ideas: budget-conditioned branch-level value-head experiment with derived compare/defer
relaxed pairwise labels on current artifacts using provenance and disagreement metadata
provenance-aware weighting across exact and approximate labels
selective relabeling by decision value on near-ties and disagreement states
safe_wording: The most promising target-design direction is to model next-step branch allocation as budget-conditioned selective marginal allocation, using branch-level continuation-value signals, softer or structured supervision on ambiguous pairs, and explicit defer/unresolved handling rather than forcing all hard comparisons into binary winner labels.
not_yet_safe: branch-level value supervision is already the settled canonical winner
partial-order or abstention formulations are already empirically closed
raw predicted value magnitude can already be trusted as confidence
recommended_next_step: Run a bounded auditable experiment on one of: branch-level budget-conditioned value prediction with derived compare/defer, relaxed pairwise targets tied to provenance and disagreement, or partial-order/unresolved treatment on hardest slices.
